message.content: We need to match messy company names across two CRMs. Some differ by punctuation, Unicode accents, or typos. Suggest an approach.

content.text: I chose Levenshtein distance over Jaro-Winkler because it handles the typo cases we're seeing more consistently. I rejected naive substring matching because it fails on accented characters. The approach: normalize Unicode with NFKD, strip punctuation, then compute Levenshtein and accept matches under a threshold of 3 edits.
I considered n-gram/trigram similarity but rejected it because our names are short (often 1-2 tokens) so trigram overlap is noisy. Levenshtein is more reliable at this length.
Implementing in src/match/fuzzy.ts with the NFKD-normalize + Levenshtein pipeline.
